title: atof, atoi and atol from <stdlib.h> should be avoided
description: "atof", "atoi" and "atol" from <stdlib.h> should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalreliabilityBugbased-on-misracertWhy is this an issue?More Info<stdlib.h>'satof,atoi, andatolfunctions, which convert strings to numbers, have undefined behavior when the strings cannot be converted, and should therefore be avoided.Noncompliant code exampleint converter (const char * numstr) { return atoi(numstr); // Noncompliant }Compliant solutionint converter (const char * numstr) { return strtol(numstr, NULL, 10); }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant:
#include <stdlib.h>
int result = atoi("123"); // Noncompliant: undefined behavior on invalid input
double value = atof("3.14"); // Noncompliant: no error handling
long number = atol("999999"); // Noncompliant: unsafe conversion
compliant:
#include <string>
int result = std::stoi("123"); // Compliant: C++ string conversion

checks.message: atof, atoi and atol from stdlib.h should be avoided - use std::stof, std::stoi, std::stol instead.
